summary: Decompiled and improved a large slice of Rainbow Cruise ground/stage code in `src/melee/gr/grrcruise.c`, with supporting type/header work. The PR replaced many `/// #grRCruise_*` targets with C, added Rainbow Cruise stage data and map/joint tables, recovered Ground union overlays for scroll/map/vanish state, and exposed `grAnime_801C7BA0` for cross-file use. The decomp.dev bot reported +2004 matched bytes, 6 new 100% matches, and 11 additional grrcruise improvements. There was no PR body and no human review discussion in the slice, so intent is inferred from diff and bot/merge metadata.

key_files.role: Main decomp work; 716 additions and 33 deletions, replacing numerous grRCruise target stubs and adding stage data/config tables.
Recovered/expanded Ground overlays and Rainbow Cruise structs, including `grRCruise_VanishEntry`, `grRCruise_VanishDesc`, `ScrollVars`, and map/vanish aliases.
Typed previously unknown prototypes for `grRCruise_80201288` and `grRCruise_80201988`.
Removed stale local/static forward declaration for `grAnime_801C7BA0` after moving the declaration to the header.

what_changed: Added Rainbow Cruise stage metadata/data in `grrcruise.c`, including `S16Vec3 grRc_803E4DA8[]` and `struct StageData grRc_803E4ECC` for `RCRUISE` and `/GrRc.dat`.
Expanded the static `grRc_804D6A10` configuration pointer from one `int x0` to a typed layout of float and s32 fields used by platform/vanish/rotation timing logic.

Implemented previously target-only grrcruise functions including `grRCruise_801FF924`, `grRCruise_801FFADC`, `grRCruise_80200154`, `grRCruise_80200578`, `grRCruise_8020071C`, `grRCruise_80200C04`, `grRCruise_80201110`, `grRCruise_80201288`, `grRCruise_80201410`, `grRCruise_80201588`, and `grRCruise_80201988`.

Improved existing partially decompiled functions such as `grRCruise_801FF2C8`, `grRCruise_801FF7A4`, `grRCruise_80200074`, and `grRCruise_80201918` with matching-oriented local variables, exact line constants, stack padding, and typed field access.
Changed allocation/assert logic in `grRCruise_801FF5B4` from raw `gp->gv.rcruise.entries` plus `__assert` to the recovered alias `gp->u.map.chikuwa` plus `HSD_ASSERT(0x19A, gp->u.map.chikuwa)`.
Added vanish-object initialization/update logic using `grRc_803E5014`, `gp->u.map.vanish`, `Camera_8003118C`, `grAnime_801C7FF8`, `mpJointListAdd`, `mpLib_80057BC0`, and DObj flag recursion.
Added/typed `grRCruise_80201288` as a recursive `HSD_JObj`/`HSD_DObj` traversal helper taking a callback like `HSD_DObjSetFlags` or `HSD_DObjClearFlags`.
Exposed `grAnime_801C7BA0` in `granime.h` and removed its local declaration from `granime.c` so grrcruise can call it through the shared header.

Recovered more structure fields in `types.h`: `grRCruise_Entry.x10` became `f32`, `grRCruise_GroundVars.x0C` became `s32`, vanish structs were added, `ScrollVars` was introduced, and the `Ground` union gained `scroll`.
smart_moves: Moved from raw offsets and one-field placeholder structs toward typed Ground overlays, making later stage decomp easier and improving readability without overnaming unknown fields.
Used `ARRAY_SIZE(grRc_803E4DA8)` in `StageData`, avoiding a hard-coded count for the Rainbow Cruise map/joint table.
Made `grAnime_801C7BA0` a proper public header declaration instead of relying on a file-local prototype, which is the reusable pattern when another module starts calling an existing engine function.
Kept exact assert/report line constants such as `0x122`, `0x19A`, `0x2B0`, `0x2B2`, `0x5AD`, and `0x5B3`, which matters for matching codegen and diagnostics.
Used matching-specific scaffolding shown in the diff, including `PAD_STACK`, explicit local aliases such as `stage_gobj`, and careful temporary ordering in functions that reached 100%.
Factored repeated visibility/flag behavior through recursive `HSD_JObj` traversal with a DObj callback, reusing the same helper for set/clear flag operations in vanish handling.

Modeled stage behavior through existing engine helpers (`Ground_801C3FA4`, `Ground_801C2BA4`, `grAnime_*`, `mpLib_*`, `Camera_8003118C`, `Stage_GetCamBoundsBottomOffset`) instead of open-coding lower-level object access.
decomp_lessons: Stage functions often only become tractable after the `Ground` union overlays are recovered; here `u.map`, `u.scroll`, `gv.rcruise`, and vanish/chikuwa aliases unlocked clearer code.
When a target function consumes a previously static-looking helper from another file, promote the prototype to the shared header and remove duplicate local declarations.
For matching, preserve small details that look cosmetic in source: assert macros, source line constants, local variable aliases, ternary structure, stack padding, and exact float constants.

Recursive JObj tree traversal is codegen-sensitive; the merge history notes earlier difficulty with recursive traversal/register/control-flow matching, while the final bot report shows `grRCruise_80201288` eventually reached 100%.
Use typed-but-unnamed offset fields (`x00`, `x04`, etc.) when semantics are not proven; this PR improved layouts without inventing descriptive names beyond evidence.
Bot reports are valuable for triage: this PR landed both finished matches and partial improvements, so future work should distinguish 100% functions from functions still below full match.
naming_conventions: Rainbow Cruise functions use the `grRCruise_` prefix with address-based suffixes, e.g. `grRCruise_80201288`.
Rainbow Cruise data symbols use the shorter `grRc_` prefix, e.g. `grRc_803E4DA8`, `grRc_803E4ECC`, `grRc_803E5014`, and `grRc_804D6A10`.
Stage enum/file naming in this slice is `RCRUISE` and `/GrRc.dat`.
Recovered structs keep module-specific names when clear: `grRCruise_Entry`, `grRCruise_SubEntry`, `grRCruise_VanishEntry`, and `grRCruise_VanishDesc`.
Unknown fields remain offset-style (`x00`, `x04`, `x0C`, `pad_01`) rather than speculative semantic names.
Headers retain address comments beside prototypes, e.g. `/* 201288 */` and `/* 201988 */`, while replacing `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` with concrete signatures when known.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inserted `PAD_STACK(8)`, `PAD_STACK(0x18)`, and `PAD_STACK(16)` in several grrcruise functions to match stack layout.
Used exact math/library calls and constants: `sqrtf`, `atan2f`, `cosf`, `sinf`, `0.017453292f`, `1.0471976f`, and explicit integer casts for angle wrapping.

Adjusted control flow in `grRCruise_801FF7A4` by introducing `stage_gobj`, direct `user_data`/`hsd_obj` locals, a separate `archive = grDatFiles_801C6324()` assignment, and direct use of `Ground_801C3FA4(stage_gobj, 23)`.
Preserved matching-sensitive diagnostics, including `OSReport("%s:%d: couldn t get gobj(id=%d)\n", __FILE__, 0x122, gobj_id)` and line-numbered `HSD_ASSERT` calls.
Used callback-parameterized recursion in `grRCruise_80201288` around `HSD_JObjGetDObj` to match JObj/DObj traversal patterns.
Relied on `HSD_Randi`/`HSD_Randf` and modulo-based state machines for platform timing rather than simplifying behavior into higher-level abstractions.
The decomp.dev result confirms exact matches for `grRCruise_80201988`, `grRCruise_80201288`, `grRCruise_80201110`, `grRCruise_801FF7A4`, `grRCruise_801FF2C8`, and `grRCruise_80200074`.

review_feedback.feedback: Reported 11 additional improvements, including `grRCruise_80200C04` to 90.59%, `grRCruise_8020071C` to 84.70%, `grRCruise_80200154` to 91.81%, `.data` to 84.20%, `grRCruise_801FF924` to 97.76%, and `grRCruise_801FFADC` to 29.40%.
No human review comments or reviews were present; the PR body was empty.

follow_up_queries: Search later PRs for `grRCruise_801FFADC` because this slice only improved it to 29.40%.
Search later PRs for `grRCruise_80201588` and Rainbow Cruise vanish handling because this slice reported only 60.09% for that function.
Search `grRc_804D6A10` to see whether the recovered configuration fields were later named or further typed.
Search `ScrollVars` and `u.scroll` for reuse in other moving/scrolling stage decomp work.
Search `grAnime_801C7BA0` consumers to confirm the public header signature remained stable.
Search `grRCruise_80201288` for any later cleanup of the recursive traversal now that the bot reported a 100% match.
